🤖 Agentic workflow · Sysco foodservice

Smart Assignment

An AI agent that autonomously recommends the best delivery day & time slot for a new customer — running every step end-to-end, enforcing hard operational rules in code, ranking options on weighted business factors, and escalating to a human only when it isn't confident.

🤖 Fully agent-automated ✅ Deterministic, auditable decisions 🧠 LLM-written reasoning 🧪 Running on mock data
The problem

Onboarding a new account, every time, the same way

When a new foodservice customer signs on, someone has to decide which delivery route and time window they should join. That decision balances truck capacity, geography, and the customer's preference — and it needs to be consistent, explainable, and fast. The Smart Assignment agent makes that call automatically, and flags the tricky ones for a specialist instead of guessing.

How the agent works

Five steps, executed autonomously by the agent

🤖
Every step below is performed by the AI agent — no human in the loop. The agent orchestrates the whole flow end-to-end; a person is involved only if the agent decides to escalate at the final step.
🤖 Agent
1

Intake

Capture the customer's address, order quantity (cases), and preferred window.

Agent validates the number & builds the profile.

🤖 Agent
2

Geo-Lookup

Geocode the address and find the nearest candidate routes.

Agent geocodes & picks the Top-3.

🤖 Agent
3

Constraint Check

Drop any route that fails a hard rule.

Agent enforces serviceability, capacity, window.

🤖 Agent
4

Score & Rank

Rank survivors on weighted business factors.

Agent scores & orders the options.

🤖 Agent
5

Recommend

Return the top slot with a reasoning trace — or escalate.

Agent self-scores confidence & decides.

Architecture

The agentic workflow, end to end

The agent is a Google ADK Workflow that orchestrates the five steps. It calls deterministic tools for the objective checks and an LLM only to narrate its decision — the decision itself is code, so it's reproducible and auditable.

🤖

Agent orchestrator

An ADK Workflow drives all five steps and the branching, autonomously.

🧭

Deterministic tools

Geo, hard constraints, and weighted scoring — plain code the agent calls.

🧠

LLM reasoner

Gemini turns the agent's decision into a plain-English rationale (optional).

🙋

Human-in-the-loop

Only engaged when the agent escalates — low confidence or no feasible slot.

The rules the agent enforces

Hard constraints — non-negotiable, checked in code

These are objective facts, not judgment calls. The agent removes any route that fails one before ranking — it can never "reason" a customer onto a full truck or outside the serviceable area.

📍

Geographic serviceability

The customer must fall within the route's serviceable radius.

📦

Route capacity

The truck stays at or below 90% capacity after adding this order.

🕑

Delivery-window fit

The route offers a window overlapping the customer's preference, if stated.

How the agent ranks the rest

Weighted scoring factors

Among the routes that pass every hard rule, the agent uses these weighted factors to decide the winner. Weights reflect priority and are fully configurable.

🧭

Geographic clustering

How tightly the customer clusters with the route's existing stops — tighter clusters mean more predictable arrivals.

weight 0.45
🛡️

Capacity buffer

How much headroom is left after the add — more buffer means a route that's resilient to day-of disruption.

weight 0.30
🎯

Window match

How well the route's window covers the customer's stated preference.

weight 0.25
The outcome

Three possible agent decisions

Every run ends in one of three states. Anything below a 70% confidence threshold, or with no valid slot at all, the agent hands to a human — with full context attached.

✔ Recommended

A clear winner above the confidence threshold — the agent auto-assigns.

⚠ Low confidence

A slot is proposed, but the options are close — the agent asks a specialist to confirm.

✖ No feasible slot

Every candidate failed a hard rule — the agent routes it to a specialist.

Try it yourself

Run the agent on a customer

Enter a mock customer number (or pick one below) and watch the agent execute each step, then render its recommendation. Everything runs in your browser — no data leaves the page.

All outcomes at a glance

What the agent decided for each mock customer

These cards are generated straight from the agent's output. Each customer lands on a different outcome; expand “Routes the agent evaluated” to audit the full decision.

067-100001

Bayou City Bistro

1200 McKinney St, Houston, TX 77010
90 cases prefers 07:00–10:00 3 routes evaluated
✔ Recommended agent confidence 89%

→ RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 07:00–10:00 (score 0.77)

Geographic clustering
0.94
Capacity buffer
0.32
Window match
1.00
Why the agent chose thisRecommending RTE-4100 (Central Houston) on TUE, window 07:00-10:00, score 0.77. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 0.8 mi to existing stops); window_match (180/180 min of preferred window covered); capacity_buffer (290 cases headroom (68% utilized post-add)). Confidence 89%.
Routes the agent evaluated (3)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 0.1 miFEASIBLE · 0.77
  • ✓ serviceability: 0.1 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 68% post-add (limit 90%), 290 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 180 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:00-10:00)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 14.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 14.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 52% post-add (limit 90%), 460 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 150 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 18.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 18.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 101% post-add (limit 90%), -10 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 06:00-09:00)
067-100002

Galleria Grill & Catering

5085 Westheimer Rd, Houston, TX 77056
140 cases no window preference 3 routes evaluated
⚠ Low confidence — human review agent confidence 51%
below the confidence threshold

→ proposed: RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 07:30–11:00 (score 0.58)

Geographic clustering
0.67
Capacity buffer
0.43
Window match
0.60
Why the agent escalatedRecommending RTE-4200 (West Houston / Energy Corridor) on WED, window 07:30-11:00, score 0.58. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 5.0 mi to existing stops); window_match (no stated preference (neutral score)); capacity_buffer (410 cases headroom (57% utilized post-add)). Passed over: RTE-4100/TUE scored 0.52 (−0.06). Confidence 51% is below the 70% threshold (options are close / scores modest) — flagging for human review before committing.
Routes the agent evaluated (3)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 5.9 miFEASIBLE · 0.52
  • ✓ serviceability: 5.9 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 73% post-add (limit 90%), 240 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 9.3 miFEASIBLE · 0.58
  • ✓ serviceability: 9.3 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 57% post-add (limit 90%), 410 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 13.3 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 13.3 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 109% post-add (limit 90%), -60 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: no stated preference; any available window acceptable
067-100003

Katy Prairie Steakhouse

24600 Katy Fwy, Katy, TX 77494
260 cases prefers 06:00–08:00 3 routes evaluated
✖ No feasible slot — specialist agent confidence 0%
no candidate passed the hard rules
Why the agent escalatedNo feasible slot for Katy Prairie Steakhouse: every candidate route was ruled out by a hard constraint [RTE-4200 (WED): failed geographic_serviceability; RTE-4400 (TUE): failed geographic_serviceability, route_capacity; RTE-4100 (TUE): failed geographic_serviceability]. Escalating to a routing specialist for a manual decision (new route, schedule change, or capacity reallocation).
Routes the agent evaluated (3)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 12.8 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 12.8 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 69% post-add (limit 90%), 290 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 30 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4400 · Southwest / Sugar Land · TUE · 16.0 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 16.0 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✗ capacity: utilization 126% post-add (limit 90%), -180 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 06:00-09:00)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 27.4 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 27.4 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 87% post-add (limit 90%), 120 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 60 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:00-10:00)

✔ Recommended agent confidence 93%

→ RTE-4300 · North Houston / The Woodlands · THU · 08:00–12:00 (score 0.85)

Geographic clustering
0.96
Capacity buffer
0.56
Window match
1.00
Why the agent chose thisRecommending RTE-4300 (North Houston / The Woodlands) on THU, window 08:00-12:00, score 0.85. Key factors: geographic_clustering (avg 0.6 mi to existing stops); window_match (180/180 min of preferred window covered); capacity_buffer (450 cases headroom (44% utilized post-add)). Confidence 93%.
Routes the agent evaluated (3)
RTE-4300 · North Houston / The Woodlands · THU · 0.3 miFEASIBLE · 0.85
  • ✓ serviceability: 0.3 mi from route center (limit 16.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 44% post-add (limit 90%), 450 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 180 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 08:00-12:00)
RTE-4200 · West Houston / Energy Corridor · WED · 27.7 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 27.7 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 58% post-add (limit 90%), 400 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 120 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 07:30-11:00)
RTE-4100 · Central Houston · TUE · 28.4 miINFEASIBLE
  • ✗ serviceability: 28.4 mi from route center (limit 12.0 mi)
  • ✓ capacity: utilization 74% post-add (limit 90%), 230 cases headroom
  • ✓ window: 90 min overlap with preferred window (best route window 10:30-12:30)
About this data. The agent runs on mock Houston-area routes and geocoding so the workflow can be demonstrated end-to-end. Capacities, service radii, scoring weights, and thresholds are illustrative starting points — not validated Sysco policy. The route source and geocoder are designed to be swapped for real systems without changing the agent's logic.
Smart Assignment · an AI agent for delivery slot recommendation · built on Google's Agent Development Kit (ADK).
Source & docs on GitHub. Decisions are deterministic and auditable; reasoning narration is LLM-written with a deterministic fallback.
